author: @DM-kl4em
text:
I love it!  I'm a mechanical engineer by trade, so I have a strong partiality to purely mechanical devices.

It would also be very entertaining to incorporate a "weight battery" into such a device.  A hand crank is MUCH more practical, but some experiments are just fun, and good for exercising the engineering brain.

author: @hubrisnaut
text: When I was a kid we made ice cream with a hand cranked churn. I remember my grandmother still had her butter churn. Have we reinvented the wheel for fun?

author: @Andrew-h3q6b
text: Rather than connecting the drive directly to the blender, maybe, connect it to a drill chuck. That way you could use it for different things. One minute a blender, next a generator or battery charger. Even a fan or, (with gear reduction) a water pump. If you could make a version that what lighter and smaller, It might become something to take camping?

replies.author: @DM-kl4em
replies.text: I hear you.  I have a purely mechanical hand crank drill.  If I am putting in decking boards or fence rails, I sometimes put a drill bit on the hand crank drill and screwdriving head on my electric drill.  Not having to change bits every time gives me an excuse to use the hand crank drill just for fun.
